Document:
# Roleplay Rule 2 - Escalation and Roleplay Violence may be initiated without prior warning if it is grounded in coherent character motivation. Premeditated or surprise attacks (for example, stalking, leaving messages, building tension, or planning a targeted killing) are permitted, even if they appear sudden to others, provided they are part of an intentional roleplay arc and not arbitrary disruption. (Though we permit more freedom in regards to escalation and self-antagonism, we still expect players to have some level of IC justification for their actions and to not just randomly attack people without reason.)
If you are attacked, you may defend yourself — including with lethal force. Self-defense is permitted when proportionate to the threat. The level of force used must somewhat reasonably reflect the danger presented. Players who initiate conflict without legitimate IC justification, such as theft or aggression without an argument, may not claim lethal self-defense if reasonable or excessive force is used against them in response.
Escalation used as a shortcut to only create chaos or bypass roleplay expectations is not permitted.
